Wires and poles for carrying light

Henry Root was confirmed ~ city
conmissioner to succeed Henry L.

ice yesterday.


Confirmation was given to the big
Fourth ward sewer district. 1t was
stated by City Engineer Gill that out
of $50 lots only 63h were opposed to
the district being established.


The matter Of P. F. Byrne succeed
ing Allen r. Gill as city engineer
again came UP in committee, and Mr..
Oil said he had offered to pay Mr..
Byrne $3.50 out Of his own pocket,
although he had done the work. Cor
poration Counsel Judson reported an
o1snoe providing for the payment


Ner routine business was traAs<
acted.


NJ Woydt rose to a question Of
privilege and spoke at length in de-
Tense of himself and the council on
the personal attacks made by the
SpOkesman-Review on account Of the
Main street vacation deal.


Elm answering these attacks" said
Mr.. Woydt, II shall call spade a
spade. for in dealing with this treach-
erous and villainous sheet one is not
supposed to use the usual civilities.
When one is attacked by snake any
club will do In defense.


"Mr. President. In answering the
slanderous articles printed by the
Review against the members of this
council. l shall not explain my ac-
tions, as l have no explanation or
excuses to make. Anyone at all con-
versant with business methods and
rules governing this council will know
that this council can act only by mo-
tion or resolution, reference to and
reports of committees and the pass
age OF ordinances, therefore the rec
ord shows every move we have made.


"In answer to published statements
I wish to say they are falsehoods
from beginning to end. In this case
the motive for the Misrepresentation
is apparent. and state 1t to show
that there is one man who dares to
say his soul Is his own and who
neither fears the papers influence or
asks a favor of it, but does demand
Justice.


'The Review had an opportunity to
be heard on March g. the date set by
this council for the hearing of pro-
tests and objections against the pro-
posed Main street vacation deal. This
matter has been before the council
for months and this papers reporters
were present at each meeting. and
1t kept close watch Of what was being
done.


'Tf the public is being defrauded.
as the Review says. why has the
paper been silent all these weeks
and months? Why does It withhold
its protest and acquiesce in the pro-
ceedings until after the matter has
